name: San Felices Castle and Lumbrales Loop
name_original: Castillo de San Felices de los Gallegos y Lumbrales — circular Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero
description: This moderate 41.0-mile (66.0 km) road cycling route in Arribes del Duero Natural Park offers challenging climbs and dramatic canyon views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Bañobárez traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, particularly around the Duero Special Protection Area. The region offers varied terrain suitable for road cyclists, with routes often passing through rural areas and offering views of the surrounding countryside. Elevation gains indicate a moderately challenging environment, with climbs and descents common throughout the network. Best road cycling routes around Bañobárez The most popular road cycling route is San Felices Castle and Lumbrales Loop, a 41.0 miles (66.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This route features rolling terrain and passes through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Almeida and Puerto Seguro - circular route through the Duero Special Protection Area, a moderate 35.6 miles (57.3 km) path. It offers scenic views within a protected natural area. Local road cyclists also love the La Alameda de Gardón – Gallegos de Argañán loop from Villar de Ciervo, a 28.7 miles (46.2 km) trail leading through agricultural lands and small villages, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Bañobárez is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and rural roads. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with 20 road cycling routes available. The routes in Bañobárez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bañobárez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Bañobárez?
A: The region around Bañobárez is characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, particularly near the Duero Special Protection Area. You'll find varied terrain with climbs and descents, often passing through rural landscapes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Bañobárez?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Bañobárez, offering a range of options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Bañobárez?
A: While most routes in Bañobárez are rated moderate to difficult due to the rolling terrain and elevation changes, there are no routes specifically classified as easy. Cyclists should be prepared for some climbs.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Bañobárez area?
A: For a more challenging ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Seguro – Turones River, Escarigo loop from Castillejo de Martín Viejo&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 84 kilometers long with nearly 1000 meters of elevation gain, offering a significant test for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Bañobárez?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026442372/almeida-and-puerto-seguro-circular-route-through-the-duero-special-protection-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeida and Puerto Seguro - circular route through the Duero Special Protection Area&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 57.3 km loop offering scenic views.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of road cycling routes in Bañobárez?
A: Routes vary in length, but many fall within the 40 to 70-kilometer range. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Alameda de Gardón – Gallegos de Argañán loop from Villar de Ciervo&lt;/a&gt; is 46.2 kilometers and typically takes around 2 hours and 9 minutes.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Bañobárez?
A: Many routes pass through rural areas with views of the countryside. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4321091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of San Felices de los Gallegos&lt;/a&gt; or natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6517238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Dos Casas Stream&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also go through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lumbrales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through protected natural areas?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026442372/almeida-and-puerto-seguro-circular-route-through-the-duero-special-protection-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeida and Puerto Seguro - circular route through the Duero Special Protection Area&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views within this protected natural environment.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Bañobárez?
A: The road cycling routes in Bañobárez are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain and the opportunity to explore rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to look out for?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can find historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6193276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Franceses&lt;/a&gt;, a notable bridge, or explore the small village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bogajo&lt;/a&gt;, depending on your chosen route.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Bañobárez?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be hot, while winters are cooler, so checking local weather conditions is always advisable.
tips:
The castle tower that still stands was part of the fortress built in the 13th century by the Portuguese king Don Dinis to protect the lands in the area.
It is advisable to visit the tower inside, it houses a Historical Interpretation Classroom and a permanent exhibition on the Border Fortifications. In addition, from the top floor you can enjoy beautiful views of the entire area, including neighboring Portuguese lands.

Hinojosa de Duero is a charming village in the El Abadengo region (Salamanca), located right on the border with Portugal and within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, making it an ideal destination for rural tourism, nature and gastronomy.

The city of Lumbrales is visited mainly for the ruins of Castro Vetón on its outskirts, but also in the urban area it offers several interesting places to visit. Among them, the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción and the Verraco Vetón stand out in front of it and a few steps from the Plaza Mayor.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular road cycling route is approximately 66 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty, requiring good to very good fitness. The Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero features a rugged, hilly terrain with significant ascents and descents, making it challenging for novice road cyclists.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: As a circular route connecting San Felices de los Gallegos and Lumbrales, you can start from either town. Both San Felices de los Gallegos and Lumbrales are good starting points and typically offer parking options within the villages.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling in the Arribes del Duero Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by a rugged, granite landscape with deep canyons and steep cliffs. Expect significant ascents and descents throughout the ride. While many roads are paved, some sections, especially those leading to more remote areas, may have variable quality. This makes for a challenging but rewarding experience.

faqs.question: What are the main landmarks and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable sights. The route passes through the historic village of San Felices de los Gallegos, home to a medieval castle. You'll also cycle through Lumbrales and Hinojosa de Duero. The most striking features are the spectacular canyons of Las Arribes del Duero, offering breathtaking panoramic views where the Duero River forms a natural border with Portugal.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle in the Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero are spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling. Spring offers the beauty of blooming almond trees, while autumn provides comfortable temperatures and beautiful fall colors. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cold and wet.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other official paths. You may find yourself on parts of the R1 - Camino antiguo de La Redonda, R3 - Camino antiguo de San Felices de los Gallegos, R4 - Camino del Puente de los Franceses, and R2 - Camino de Ahigal de los Aceiteros, among others.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in the Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and private property.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Lumbrales and Hinojosa de Duero are settlements along the route where you can find restaurants, cafes, and shops to refuel and resupply.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily on public roads, and dogs are generally allowed in the Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero, it's important to consider the nature of a road cycling trip. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can safely travel with you (e.g., in a trailer) and that you adhere to all local leash laws and waste disposal regulations, especially when passing through villages.

faqs.question: What makes this road cycling route particularly notable?
faqs.answer: This route stands out for its dramatic and unique landscape, featuring the 'arribes' (steep riverbanks) of the Duero River. It combines challenging cycling with rich historical and cultural experiences, passing through traditional villages like San Felices de los Gallegos. You'll also enjoy tranquil roads with little traffic and cross-border views into Portugal.

waypoints.name: Castle of San Felices de los Gallegos
